Panax ginseng, commonly known as the ginseng plant, is a perennial plant that belongs to the family Araliaceae. Panax ginseng has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties and is valued for its health benefits, including reducing stress, improving cognitive function, and boosting the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Panax ginseng include ginsenosides, phenolic compounds, and polysaccharides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Panax ginseng is commonly used in herbal preparations such as capsules, tea, and tinctures, often in combination with other herbs to enhance its effects.
